etymology.description: 掌(掌) (zhǎng, "palm; control") refers to the palm of the hand, which is the part used to clap. 聲(聲) (shēng, "sound; voice") denotes sound, which results from clapping. The combination of these two characters refers to the sound made by the clapping of palms, which is known as "applause."
etymology.interpretation: 掌(掌) (zhǎng, "palm; control") denotes the palm of the hand. The primary sense here is the palm's role in creating sound by clapping the hands. 聲(聲) (shēng, "sound; voice") means sound. When paired with 掌(掌) (zhǎng, "palm"), it implies the sound generated when multiple palms clap together, correlated with applause. Applause derives from this clapping gesture specifically as a way for audiences to express appreciation or approval audibly.

examples.chinese: 表演结束后(表演結束後)，观众爆发出热烈的掌声(觀眾爆發出熱烈的掌聲)。
examples.pinyin: Biǎoyǎn jiéshù hòu, guānzhòng bàofā chū rèliè de zhǎngshēng.
examples.english: After the performance ended, the audience erupted into enthusiastic applause.

examples.chinese: 他的演讲赢得了全场掌声(他的演講贏得了全場掌聲)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnjiǎng yíngdé le quánchǎng zhǎngshēng.
examples.english: His speech won applause from the whole audience.

examples.chinese: 在音乐会中(在音樂會中)，钢琴家每首曲子后都收到了掌声(鋼琴家每首曲子後都收到了掌聲)。
examples.pinyin: Zài yīnyuèhuì zhōng, gāngqínjiā měi shǒu qǔzi hòu dōu shōudào le zhǎngshēng.
examples.english: During the concert, the pianist received applause after each piece.
